codingTopics: LSM-tree storage: memtable buffering with sorted flush to immutable SSTable segments (data structure, systems, infrastructure)
Consistent-hashing ring for partition placement across nodes (systems, infrastructure, dependency)
Merge sorted SSTable iterators during compaction with tombstone handling (merge, sort, streams)
Vector similarity top-K over embeddings with a bounded heap (heap, search)
Time-series wide-row range scan with TTL/tombstone skipping (time-series, logs, sliding-window)
systemDesignTopics: Design a distributed wide-column store like Cassandra with tunable consistency and multi-datacenter replication
Design Astra DB serverless vector search serving nearest-neighbor queries over billions of embeddings
Design the compaction and anti-entropy repair subsystem that keeps replicas consistent under continuous writes
Design a multi-tenant control plane that provisions isolated Cassandra clusters on demand

coding: Implement a memtable for a Cassandra-style LSM engine: buffer writes in sorted order and flush to an immutable SSTable when a size threshold is hit. Discuss compaction and the read-path merge.
Given the token ranges owned by nodes on a consistent-hashing ring, route a partition key to its replica set and explain how ranges move when a node joins or leaves.
Merge K sorted SSTable iterators into one ordered stream, applying last-write-wins and dropping tombstoned rows. Analyze memory and complexity.
Given high-dimensional embeddings in Astra DB, return the top-K nearest neighbors for a query vector using a bounded heap. Discuss approximate vs exact trade-offs.
Implement a range scan over a time-series wide row that skips expired (TTL) cells and tombstones without loading the whole partition.
behavioral: Tell me about a time you debugged a data-consistency or replication issue in a distributed system. How did you isolate it?
Describe a time you contributed to or depended on an open-source project. How did you engage the community?
Tell me about balancing a customer's urgent production issue against planned roadmap work.
Describe taking ownership of an ambiguous, under-specified problem and driving it to done.
systemDesign: Design a distributed wide-column database like Cassandra: partitioning, replication, tunable consistency (quorum), and hinted handoff.
Design Astra DB vector search: ANN indexing, sharding embeddings, and serving sub-100ms nearest-neighbor queries.
Design the anti-entropy repair and compaction pipeline that reconciles divergent replicas.
Design a multi-tenant serverless control plane that autoscales Cassandra clusters per customer.
values: DataStax's roots are in open-source Apache Cassandra. What does building in the open mean to you?
How do you weigh developer experience against raw performance when designing a database API?
Why work on data infrastructure specifically rather than product features?
other: How does DataStax compensation compare to MongoDB?

rawSummary:
DataStax Interview Guide (2026): Cassandra and AI Data Engineering
By 			
				
				techinterview.org			
			
		

		 /  May 5, 2026 
			
		

	
	
	

		
		
On this page

- Process

- What they actually ask

- Levels and comp (2026)

- Prep priorities

- Frequently Asked Questions

Updated May 5, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

DataStax
datastax.com ↗

DataStax is the steward of Apache Cassandra and now ships Astra DB (managed Cassandra) plus generative AI tooling for RAG and vector search. The interview is database-internals heavy and rewards engineers with real distributed-systems chops.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding phone (DSA medium) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Senior+ candidates get an additional distributed-systems round. Cycle: 3–4 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design a globally distributed key-value store with tunable consistency

- Design a vector search engine that integrates with Cassandra

- Implement consistent hashing with virtual nodes and rebalancing

- Coding: graph/tree problems with distributed flavor

- Distributed systems: gossip protocol, hinted handoff, read repair, anti-entropy

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $160K–$200K total

- Senior SE: $230K–$300K

- Staff: $330K–$430K

- Principal: $450K–$580K

Prep priorities

- Read the Cassandra paper (Lakshman/Malik) and Dynamo paper

- Understand vector search basics: HNSW, IVF, ANN tradeoffs

- Be fluent in Java — Cassandra is Java; tooling is Go and Python

Frequently Asked Questions

Is DataStax fully remote?

Distributed-first with hubs in Santa Clara, London, and Sydney. Most engineering roles are remote-eligible.

What is the AI angle?

DataStax bought Langflow and shipped Astra Vector Search. Roles in AI/ML platform are growing fast.

How does DataStax compensation compare to MongoDB?

MongoDB pays more on cash and equity. DataStax base is competitive but equity is private/late-stage and harder to value.
